title: Transfer Impact Assessment currency — stale Schrems II assessments
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate the substantive quality of a TIA; surfaces only TIAs that have not been reviewed within policy cadence.
evidence: Saved search 'tia_currency' (weekly) lists TIAs past their review date with the related transfer flows.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.46 SCC safeguards rely on a maintained TIA; detecting staleness is a direct operational control.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that GDPR Art.46 (Transfers subject to safeguards) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.38.4: Transfer Impact Assessment currency — stale Schrems II assessments.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.38.4' running on TIA register (tia_register.csv), transfer_register.csv, DPO review events., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: UK equivalent to Art.46 is maintained through TIAs (ICO International Data Transfer Agreement).
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that UK-GDPR Art.46 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.38.4: Transfer Impact Assessment currency — stale Schrems II assessments.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.38.4' running on TIA register (tia_register.csv), transfer_register.csv, DPO review events., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Derived from GDPR@2016/679 Art.46 (parent assurance='full'). UK GDPR preserves parent clause numbering; inherited assurance degraded one step.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that UK GDPR Art.46 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.38.4: Transfer Impact Assessment currency — stale Schrems II assessments.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.38.4' running on TIA register (tia_register.csv), transfer_register.csv, DPO review events., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A TIA last reviewed 15 months ago (policy cadence: 12 months) appears in the next weekly run.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A TIA reviewed within the last 12 months does not appear.

dataSources: TIA register (tia_register.csv), transfer_register.csv, DPO review events.
spl:
| inputlookup tia_register.csv
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(last_reviewed,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where age_days>cadence_days
| lookup transfer_register.csv tia_id OUTPUT transfer_id destination
| table tia_id destination last_reviewed age_days cadence_days transfer_id
description: Makes TIA currency a visible metric rather than a yearly memory item. Stale TIAs are the most common Schrems II finding by supervisory authorities.
value: Converts a calendar reminder into a demonstrable control with evidence per-transfer.
implementation: (1) Maintain tia_register.csv; (2) run weekly; (3) on miss, DPO re-opens the TIA review workflow.
visualization: Table of stale TIAs with remediation owner, bar chart of TIAs by age bucket, single-value 'TIAs within cadence'.
knownFalsePositives: TIAs under active review (with a workflow_state='in_review' flag) should be excluded via workflow_state.
grandmaExplanation: Converts a calendar reminder into a demonstrable control with evidence -transfer — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: TIA register (tia_register.csv), transfer_register.csv, DPO review events..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain tia_register.csv; (2) run weekly; (3) on miss, DPO re-opens the TIA review workflow.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup tia_register.csv
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(last_reviewed,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where age_days>cadence_days
| lookup transfer_register.csv tia_id OUTPUT transfer_id destination
| table tia_id destination last_reviewed age_days cadence_days transfer_id
```

Understanding this SPL

**Transfer Impact Assessment currency — stale Schrems II assessments** — Converts a calendar reminder into a demonstrable control with evidence per-transfer.

Documented **Data sources**: TIA register (tia_register.csv), transfer_register.csv, DPO review events. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where age_days>cadence_days` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Pipeline stage (see **Transfer Impact Assessment currency — stale Schrems II assessments**): table tia_id destination last_reviewed age_days cadence_days transfer_id


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of stale TIAs with remediation owner, bar chart of TIAs by age bucket, single-value 'TIAs within cadence'.
